content:
Incident Report
Page 6 of 11
Task Force. Gocha was wearing khaki pants, blue shirt, ballcap, black shoes, a duty belt with a firearm in a holster, a soft armor vest, and a ballistic plate carrier with miscellaneous equipment attached.
I took 360 degree photographs of Gocha as he stood with his equipment on as he said he had it during the incident. I had Gocha remove (2) AR magazines from his plate carrier. I counted 30 rifle rounds in each magazine with Detective Chatterton verifying the count. The magazines were reloaded with the ammunition. I then had Gocha remove his handgun from his holster, which he then provided to me. The handgun is a Glock 17 9mm (SN: BGFE262) . I removed the magazine from the handgun, and I observed a 9mm round in the chamber upon locking the slide to the rear. I counted (19) 9mm rounds from the magazine for a total of (20) rounds. The rounds were placed into a separate bag, which were then placed into a box that the handgun was secured into. I then removed an extra pistol magazine from Gocha's duty belt, which consisted of (20) 9mm rounds.
I asked Gocha if he had all of his equipment currently with him or if some of it was still at the scene. Gocha said he has a pistol magazine somewhere at the scene. He also said his rifle was not used, however, it would still be inside a Chevy Traverse, which was driven by Ryan Kimmel. Gocha said he is unaware where that vehicle is currently at.
I continued to photograph and document Gocha's equipment as Chatterton was placing them into evidence bags. I also collected Gocha's clothing, which included his wrist watch and shoes, which had blood on them. Gocha's personal wallet and chap stick were the only personal items that were photographed and returned to Gocha.
While we were on scene, we were advised that some of the witness officers would not be providing a statement at that time.
I was then tasked with processing Erik Clarkson as a witness officer. Clarkson informed me he is a Sergeant with the Pierce County Sheriff's Department, as well as a full-time task force operator with the US Marshall Service. I took 360 degree photographs of Clarkson and of his equipment. Clarkson said his rifle was left in the backseat of a minivan at the scene of the incident. He described his rifle as a Daniel Defense SWAT rifle with a 4 inch suppressor with a multi-cam cover. He also informed me there was a Noise Flash Diversionary Device (NFDD) in the front cupholder of the vehicle.
I had Clarkson remove his handgun from his duty belt holster so I could photograph it. The handgun is a Glock 17 9mm (SN: BBBT899) . I counted one live 9mm round in the chamber, as well as (22) rounds in the magazine. I also counted the live rounds in two extra handgun magazines. One magazine had 21 rounds and the other had 22 rounds. I also had Clarkson remove an AR magazine from his duty belt. I counted (20) rifle rounds from the magazine. Each magazine was reloaded with the ammunition and provided back to Clarkson.
I was also tasked with processing Ryan Kimmel as a witness officer. I also took 360 degree photographs of Kimmel and of his equipment. Kimmel informed me his rifle was left in the front seat of the Chevy Traverse he had been driving. His rifle was described as a "Colt" with (SN: LE008694) .
rplwdir.x1
09/14/20
